topics.title: Month-End Close Benchmarks 2026: How Fast Should Your Firm Close Client Books?
topics.angle: 2026 survey data (FloQast/BlackLine/Numeric) says reconciliation is the #1 bottleneck for 60-70% of teams and multi-entity consolidation adds 2-5 days. Give firms a per-client-complexity benchmark table and a repeatable checklist that states hours saved per client per month.
topics.rationale: Fresh 2026 benchmark data to cite; close acceleration is FinBoard's core CTA. Existing posts cover the 5-day close playbook but not benchmarks. Target prompt: 'how long should month end close take for a small business'

topics.title: Chart of Accounts Standardization Across Your QBO Client Base
topics.angle: Firms can't benchmark or consolidate clients when every file has a different COA. A mapping-layer approach standardizes reporting without touching client books — exactly what FinBoard's account mapping does.
topics.rationale: Listed persona pain with zero existing coverage; direct product fit (mapping/reporting model). Target prompt: 'how to standardize chart of accounts across quickbooks clients'

topics.title: 100% Bonus Depreciation Is Back: Recording OBBBA Changes in QuickBooks Online
topics.angle: OBBBA restored 100% bonus depreciation effective 2026 (was 40% in 2025). A dated, deadline-anchored walkthrough: which clients benefit, the fixed-asset and depreciation entries in QBO, and the book-vs-tax gap to track.
topics.rationale: Most searched OBBBA change for practitioners this year; nothing on-site covers OBBBA. Target prompt: 'is bonus depreciation 100% in 2026 and how do I book it'

topics.title: Q3 Estimated Taxes Due September 15: How to Calculate Yours From QuickBooks
topics.angle: Jargon-free, worked-example guide: safe-harbor rules (90% current year / 100-110% prior year), pulling YTD net income from a QBO P&L, and a small real-number example ahead of the Sept 15, 2026 deadline.
topics.rationale: Deadline-anchored seasonal search that peaks Jul-Sep; recurring persona pain; no existing tax-deadline content. Target prompt: 'how much should I pay for Q3 estimated taxes'

topics.title: Why Your P&L Shows Profit but Your Bank Account Disagrees
topics.angle: The classic accrual-vs-cash confusion, explained with one small worked example: an invoice booked in June, paid in August, plus loan principal and owner draws that never hit the P&L.
topics.rationale: Top-of-funnel evergreen answering the persona's #1 typed question; existing cash-flow posts are CFO-level (13-week forecast), not beginner. Target prompt: 'why does my profit and loss not match my bank balance'

topics.title: POS Reconciliation: Getting Toast and Square Daily Sales to Match QuickBooks
topics.angle: A benchmark-with-formula piece: the daily sales journal entry pattern, why processor fees and tips make deposits never match sales, and a weekly reconciliation routine that catches drift.
topics.rationale: Listed persona pain with zero existing coverage (the published restaurant post covers multi-location prime cost, not POS-to-books). Target prompt: 'why don't my Toast deposits match QuickBooks'

topics.title: Payer Mix Shifts in 2026: The Finance Number Every Practice Administrator Should Track
topics.angle: 2026 MPFS moves facility-based reimbursement down ~7-11% while nonfacility rises ~6%, and MA plan terminations hit 2.6M enrollees — payer mix is now a moving target. Show how to build a payer-mix revenue view from the practice's books across entities.
topics.rationale: Strong why-now (2026 fee schedule + MA churn); healthcare persona has zero published posts; multi-entity reporting is the natural CTA. Target prompt: 'how do I track payer mix for my medical practice'

topics.title: Retainage in QuickBooks Online: Tracking Holdbacks Before They Eat Your Margin
topics.angle: On a 6% margin job, retainage can exceed the entire profit. QBO has no native retainage workflow — walk the two-line invoice approach (retainage receivable asset), an aging view, and the cash-flow picture per job.
topics.rationale: Takes an artifact the contractor already produces (progress invoices) and fixes how it's recorded; construction persona has zero published posts. Target prompt: 'how to track retainage in QuickBooks Online'

topics.title: From Close to Board Deck: Automating the Monthly Board Package in 2026
topics.angle: 87% of CFOs call AI critical for 2026 and close automation cuts cycle time 40-50%. A before/after workflow: deterministic numbers from live QBO data, generated narrative for variance commentary, human review — days to hours.
topics.rationale: Cuts days off a recurring deliverable the CFO owns; sits exactly on FinBoard's live-reporting pitch; no existing board-reporting post. Target prompt: 'how to automate monthly board reporting'

topics.title: Pricing Client Advisory Services in 2026: From Hourly Compliance to Monthly CAS Revenue
topics.angle: Firms know how to bill compliance hours, not advisory value. A concrete tiering framework (close + reporting + advisory), what to include per tier, and how automated live reporting makes the advisory tier deliverable without more staff hours.
topics.rationale: Persona pain ('pricing advisory work beyond compliance billing') with strong 2026 CAS+AI momentum; distinct from the unpublished CAS Playbook draft angle by centering pricing tiers. Target prompt: 'how to price CAS services accounting firm'

approval.note: daily blog cap (2) spent 2026-07-18 by the morning publish; deploy on/after 2026-07-19 with dates re-stamped to the deploy day
